oneOf.description: Preferred input. Pass the raw Codex apply_patch payload, including the *** Begin Patch / *** End Patch envelope, as a JSON string. Nominal grammar (Lark): start: begin_patch hunk+ end_patch; begin_patch: "*** Begin Patch" LF; end_patch: "*** End Patch" LF?; hunk: add_hunk | delete_hunk | update_hunk; add_hunk: "*** Add File: " filename LF add_line+; delete_hunk: "*** Delete File: " filename LF; update_hunk: "*** Update File: " filename LF change_move? change?; filename: /(.+)/; add_line: "+" /(.*)/ LF -> line; change_move: "*** Move to: " filename LF; change: (change_context | change_line)+ eof_line?; change_context: ("@@" | "@@ " /(.+)/) LF; change_line: ("+" | "-" | " ") /(.*)/ LF; eof_line: "*** End of File" LF. Runtime notes: patch markers and file headers are trimmed, empty patch bodies parse but fail later with "No files were modified.", absolute paths are accepted, and a single update chunk supports at most one optional @@ header.

input.description: Legacy compatibility wrapper for the same raw patch string. Prefer passing the string value directly.
